description: Counter-fixture to ask-operator-returns-policy-gap: same return-policy question (which mentions a refund), but a KB article answers it. The agent has the policy, so it must reply to the customer directly and must NOT ask the merchant. Regression guard for the mutative-intent corollary: the bare word 'refund' previously stripped the reply and stood the terminal-reply guarantee down, leaving an empty plan even with a KB answer. This proves the reply survives (KB self-decay works for return/refund questions).

messages.contentText: Hi! What's your return policy? If I'm not happy with the wool throw blanket, can I send it back for a refund, and who pays the return shipping?

kbArticles.title: Returns and refunds
kbArticles.body: You can return unworn items within 30 days for a full refund. Customers cover return shipping unless the item arrived damaged or defective.

instruction: Reply to the customer about their return question.
